notes: Same opencode runner as the Rails profile — chosen for machine-readable JSON events with session IDs and token counts.
The Python profile uses prompts/benchmark_prompt_python.txt and prompts/benchmark_followup_prompt_python.txt.
Verification is performed by scripts/analyze_results_runtime_python.py: discover Django app root, install deps in a venv, boot the ASGI server, headless browser probe, docker build, docker compose.

models.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.selection_reason: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 on OpenRouter. The reference model the Python brief asks the build to wire into LangChain — running it as a benchmark target tests whether Sonnet itself can produce a working Django + Channels + LangChain app that calls Sonnet via OpenRouter.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.7
models.selection_reason: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 on OpenRouter. Tier-A baseline for the Python profile — measures whether Opus's stronger planning translates to fewer LangChain API hallucinations and a cleaner ASGI/Channels wiring than mid-tier models.

models.label: Kimi K2.6
models.selection_reason: Moonshot Kimi K2.6 via OpenRouter. In the Rails profile K2.6 hit Tier 3 by hallucinating RubyLLM's fluent API — the Python equivalent test is whether it hallucinates LangChain APIs (deprecated LLMChain, wrong import paths like langchain.chat_models vs langchain_anthropic, fabricated streaming hooks).

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.known_issues: OpenCode runs for this slug can under-ship D1/D5 vs Codex on the same Django+Channels brief (checklist scaffolding, tool configs). The benchmark runner does not build a system prompt—that is owned by OpenCode's agent; harness-side knobs are YOLO permissions, generated opencode config, and per-model opencode_model_options (reasoning=false here may reduce planning depth). Treat as model + harness sensitivity; benchmark prompt v2.1 adds an explicit deliverables checklist and SECRET_KEY guidance to reduce misses.
models.selection_reason: DeepSeek V4 Pro via OpenRouter. Phase 2 follow-up is disabled because opencode's ai-sdk strips reasoning_content but the DeepSeek API requires it echoed back, breaking multi-turn at turn 2. Phase 1 still runs end-to-end; the Python runtime analyzer (analyze_results_runtime_python.py) provides the boot/docker validation that phase 2 would otherwise do. reasoning=false tells opencode to treat it as a non-reasoning model so it does not extract/pass back reasoning_content.

models.label: MiniMax M2.7
models.selection_reason: MiniMax M2.7 via OpenRouter. Mid-tier model whose Python-stack knowledge is largely unmeasured in this harness — establishes whether MiniMax can wire LangChain + Channels correctly or hits the same fluent-DSL hallucinations seen in Rails brief Tier 2/3 outcomes.

models.label: Qwen 3.5 35B A3B
models.selection_reason: Qwen 3.5 35B A3B via OpenRouter. Agentic reasoning model with strong coding benchmarks — tests whether Qwen's tool-use reasoning carries through to correct LangChain wiring (ChatAnthropic vs ChatOpenAI, anthropic-compat endpoint, streaming hooks) on the Django + Channels brief.

models.label: GLM 5.1
models.selection_reason: GLM 5.1 via OpenRouter. Long-horizon agentic engineering model — tests whether GLM's sustained multi-turn iteration produces a complete Django + Channels + LangChain app and whether it avoids LangChain API hallucinations.
